excerpt: This mighty chapter is designated the Mother of the Book—for within it resides the light of the entire Qur'an. All subsequent chapters constitute elaborations upon this foundational sura.
source_location_hint: discussions of Surah al-Fatiha as the source of all quranic meaning

excerpt: These seven verses are recited and renewed perpetually throughout the believer's life, referred to as the seven oft-repeated verses (al-mathani).
source_location_hint: commentary on Quran 15:87 and the significance of repetition in prayer

excerpt: The Prophet declared that extensive commentary upon Surah al-Hamd demonstrates its profound depths and inexhaustible meanings.
The All-Merciful (al-Rahman) is exclusive to Allah, while the Merciful (al-Rahim) is shared among creation through divine bestowal.
source_location_hint: discussion of divine attributes and their manifestation

excerpt: Those who have incurred wrath refer to certain groups within the Ummah who deviated from the straight path, while the astray refers to others similarly misguided.
source_location_hint: esoteric interpretation of the final verses of Surah al-Fatiha

excerpt: The Prophet gave the revealed Law so that people might peel away the outer shell and grasp the inner secrets, the esoteric truths contained within.
source_location_hint: on the relationship between outer and inner meaning in revelation

excerpt: The Messenger of Allah can only be followed if his successor ʿAli is followed, establishing the principle of succession in the divine summons.
source_location_hint: on the necessity of recognizing the Imam and his proof

excerpt: Good comes from Allah; evil arises from human souls themselves, establishing the theological principle of human responsibility.
source_location_hint: on divine justice and human agency

excerpt: The seven successions of Imams are comprehensive and complete, and the Day of Resurrection comes when the seventh has passed, with the Qaʾim arriving in the interim.
source_location_hint: on the cosmic cycles and the role of the Qaʾim

excerpt: Allah has made certain people blind, deaf, and dumb to religious truth, unable to grasp even the simplest principles of the faith.
source_location_hint: on divine sealing of hearts and intellects

excerpt: The numerical values of Alif (1) and Nun (50) sum to fifty-one, signifying that the succession begun with ʿAli ends with the Qaʾim as the fifty-first.
source_location_hint: on the gematrical significance of letters and their cosmic meaning
